---
title: "ExhaustiveR"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## loading data

```{r}
# install.packages(c("readr", "dplyr", "stringr", "leaps"))
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(leaps) # We will need this for exhaustive search later

path <- "/Users/geoffrey/Desktop/Uni/2025/S2/DATA2902/Group_V1_Python/NY_House_Prices.csv"
house_prices <- read_csv(path)

# R use str_replace_all
names(house_prices) <- str_replace_all(names(house_prices), "\\.", "")

train_data <- house_prices %>% filter(Test == 0)
test_data <- house_prices %>% filter(Test == 1)

response <- 'Price'
predictors <- c('LotSize', 'Waterfront', 'Age', 'LandValue', 'NewConstruct',
                'CentralAir', 'FuelType', 'HeatType', 'SewerType', 'LivingArea','PctCollege', 'Bedrooms', 'Fireplaces', 'Bathrooms', 'Rooms')

# Convert Categorical Variables

cat_cols <- c('FuelType', 'HeatType', 'SewerType', 
              'Waterfront', 'NewConstruct', 'CentralAir') # Also include numeric categories

train_data[cat_cols] <- lapply(train_data[cat_cols], factor)
test_data[cat_cols] <- lapply(test_data[cat_cols], factor)
```

```{r}
train_data_cleaned <- train_data %>%
  mutate(
    FuelType = as.character(FuelType),  
    FuelType = na_if(FuelType, "None"), 
    HeatType = as.character(HeatType),  
    HeatType = na_if(HeatType, "None")  
  ) %>%
  na.omit() # drop all rows with any NAs

# do the exact same cleaning to the test data
test_data_cleaned <- test_data %>%
  mutate(
    FuelType = as.character(FuelType),
    FuelType = na_if(FuelType, "None"),
    HeatType = as.character(HeatType),
    HeatType = na_if(HeatType, "None")
  ) %>%
  na.omit()

# --- Re-factor the columns ---
cat_cols <- c('FuelType', 'HeatType', 'SewerType', 
              'Waterfront', 'NewConstruct', 'CentralAir')

train_data_cleaned[cat_cols] <- lapply(train_data_cleaned[cat_cols], factor)
test_data_cleaned[cat_cols] <- lapply(test_data_cleaned[cat_cols], factor)


cat(sprintf("Original train rows: %d | Cleaned train rows: %d\n", 
    nrow(train_data), nrow(train_data_cleaned)))
cat(sprintf("Original test rows: %d  | Cleaned test rows: %d\n", 
    nrow(test_data), nrow(test_data_cleaned)))


# --- Re-check the Predictor List ---

formula_obj <- as.formula(paste(response, "~", paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")))

model_matrix_r <- model.matrix(formula_obj, data = train_data_cleaned)

predictor_names_r <- colnames(model_matrix_r)[-1] 

cat("\n--- New R Predictor List (Cleaned) ---\n")
print(predictor_names_r)
cat(sprintf("\nNew total predictor count in R: %d\n", length(predictor_names_r)))

# X_train_matrix_r already exists from the code above
train_data_dummified <- as.data.frame(model_matrix_r)[, -1] # [,-1] removes intercept

# Add the response variable (Price) to it
train_data_dummified$Price <- train_data_cleaned$Price

cat("\n--- 'train_data_dummified' data frame created. Ready for lm(). ---\n")
```

## Exhaustive

```{r}

num_encoded_predictors <- length(predictor_names_r) 
n <- nrow(train_data_cleaned)

cat(sprintf("\nRunning exhaustive search on %d predictors...\n", num_encoded_predictors))

#Run the fast search with regsubsets
reg_summary <- regsubsets(formula_obj, 
                          data = train_data_cleaned, 
                          nvmax = num_encoded_predictors,
                          method = "exhaustive")

# Get the summary object
summary_stats <- summary(reg_summary)

# --- (Optional) Print the "Approximate" Best Models (from regsubsets) ---
cat("Best Models (based on *approximate* regsubsets metrics)\n")
best_bic_id_approx <- which.min(summary_stats$bic)
cat(sprintf("Best Approx. BIC (k=%d): %f\n", best_bic_id_approx, summary_stats$bic[best_bic_id_approx]))
cat("Features:\n")
print(names(which(summary_stats$outmat[best_bic_id_approx, ] == "*")))
cat("\n")


# --- Re-rank "Finalist" Models using Accurate AIC/BIC ---

# Create data frame to store the accurate metrics
# We will fit an lm() for the best model of each size k (1 to 18)
accurate_metrics <- data.frame(k = 1:num_encoded_predictors,
                               features = NA,
                               AIC = NA,
                               BIC = NA)

cat(sprintf("Re-fitting %d 'best-in-class' models for accurate metrics...\n", num_encoded_predictors))

for (k in 1:num_encoded_predictors) {
    feature_mask <- summary_stats$outmat[k, ] == "*"
    best_features_k <- names(which(feature_mask))
    
    # use backticks (`) to handle special characters/spaces in names
    best_features_k_quoted <- paste0("`", best_features_k, "`")
    formula_k_str <- paste("Price ~", paste(best_features_k_quoted, collapse = " + "))
    
    # Fit the full lm() model
    lm_k <- lm(as.formula(formula_k_str), data = train_data_dummified) # NEW    
    # Store the "accurate" AIC and BIC from the lm() object
    accurate_metrics$features[k] <- paste(best_features_k, collapse = ", ")
    accurate_metrics$AIC[k] <- AIC(lm_k)
    accurate_metrics$BIC[k] <- BIC(lm_k)
}

# Find the best model from our accurate list of 18 finalists
best_accurate_aic_id <- which.min(accurate_metrics$AIC)
best_accurate_bic_id <- which.min(accurate_metrics$BIC)

cat("--- Best Model (Lowest *Accurate* AIC) ---\n")
cat(sprintf("k = %d\n", accurate_metrics$k[best_accurate_aic_id]))
cat(sprintf("Accurate AIC: %f\n", accurate_metrics$AIC[best_accurate_aic_id]))
cat("Features:\n", accurate_metrics$features[best_accurate_aic_id], "\n")

cat("--- Best Model (Lowest *Accurate* BIC) ---\n")
cat(sprintf("k = %d\n", accurate_metrics$k[best_accurate_bic_id]))
cat(sprintf("Accurate BIC: %f\n", accurate_metrics$BIC[best_accurate_bic_id]))
cat("Features:\n", accurate_metrics$features[best_accurate_bic_id], "\n")

```

```{r}
# --- Building the selected model ---

# define selected features 
selected_features_r <- c(
    'LotSize', 
    'Waterfront1', 
    #'Age', 
    'LandValue', 
    'NewConstruct1',
    #'CentralAir1', 
    'LivingArea', 
    'Bedrooms', 
    'Bathrooms', 
    #'Rooms',
    'HeatTypeHot Air' 
    #,'Fireplaces'
)

# Build the formula string
selected_features_r_quoted <- paste0("`", selected_features_r, "`")
formula_selected_str <- paste("Price ~", paste(selected_features_r_quoted, collapse = " + "))

# Fit the OLS model using data
model_selected_r <- lm(as.formula(formula_selected_str), data = train_data_dummified)

# Print the summary
print(summary(model_selected_r))

#aic/bic
model_aic <- AIC(model_selected_r)
cat(sprintf("AIC for this model: %f\n", model_aic))

model_bic <- BIC(model_selected_r)
cat(sprintf("BIC for this model: %f\n", model_bic))
```

```{r}
# --- Calculate RMSE on Test Data ---

model_matrix_test_r <- model.matrix(formula_obj, data = test_data_cleaned)[, -1]

test_data_dummified <- as.data.frame(model_matrix_test_r)
test_data_dummified$Price <- test_data_cleaned$Price

# Define y_test
y_test_r <- test_data_dummified$Price

# Make predictions
y_pred_r <- predict(model_selected_r, newdata = test_data_dummified)

# Calculate and print RMSE
rmse_r <- sqrt(mean((y_test_r - y_pred_r)^2))

cat(sprintf("Test Data RMSE: %.2f\n", rmse_r))
```
